locationRefs: swamp road between Asineau and Cleenseau
sourceShortSummaries: After killing the dragon, the party ships off valuable remains with Vahaiya, folds a band of kobolds into Asineau's life, and expands a growing map of nearby fey realms.
Royal retainers inspect Asineau for Queen Elaine II's visit, and the party turns the hosting problem into a plan to escort her through the swamps themselves.
While escorting the queen's train through the swamps, the party recognizes the Hunter's signature work in red-eyed elk and braces for an ambush on the road.
A Duskhound reaches the entourage and seizes Queen Elaine II, but Viepuck and Izgil improvise a vertical rescue while the party beats the Hunter to death.
The surviving Duskhound tries to flee and warn Lord Umbraeth, but Robin, Celyn, Greymalkin, and Viepuck run it down before it can escape.
After the ambush, the party tells Queen Elaine II that the only real answer is to close the Feywild portal, and she orders them to do it at once.
sourceLongSummaries: The session opens in the aftermath of the dragon kill as Izgil sorts out useful dragon parts, Vahaiya carries the marketable remains back to elven lands for eventual repayment in crafted magic, and the party settles into its new role as lords of Asineau. Robin's effort to make the village more open and prosperous now includes kobold retainers and stronger ties with the local lizardfolk, while Celyn and Izgil turn their attention to a large annotated map of the Feywild and its mortal correspondences. Their work draws together what they know about Ethlenn's lands, Lord Umbraeth's reach, Greymalkin's lost realm, and new rumors from western Addermarch about another destroyed portal.

Early in October, a royal advance party arrives in Asineau to see whether the village can host the queen for a single night during her circuit through Aveil. Robin, Celyn, and the others work through sleeping arrangements, kitchens, and the awkward realities of entertaining an entourage from a poor but newly important village, while Celyn gets a small private satisfaction out of being able to produce Tyrwinghan wine from home. By the end of the discussion the real concern is security, so the party decides to ride out ahead of the queen, camp beyond the swamps, and serve as her escort on the dangerous stretch of road.

The escort begins with the party's kobolds scouting ahead of the baggage train until they spot giant deer charging in with the same red-eyed corruption the Hunter used before. The warning confirms that the attack the party feared is underway, so Robin moves to shield the entourage, Viepuck links minds with him, Izgil tracks the Hunter's spear, and Celyn slips into cover to tag the attacker with magic if he can. The opening exchange ends when the Hunter commits to the fight and teleports into melee after throwing his spear at the guards.

The ambush escalates when a Duskhound teleports into the royal ring and grabs the queen after the Hunter has already broken into the guard. Robin keeps pressure on the Hunter despite a spear wound, the guards try to keep the lesser threat contained, and the whole fight suddenly turns on whether the queen can be pulled clear before the Duskhound escapes with her. Viepuck solves that problem with startling audacity by having Es*tiasilos deliver Fly, then Izgil follows up with Dimension Door straight upward so the queen is hauled out of the melee and away from the extraction attempt. Deprived of his prize and roaring orders to warn Lord Umbraeth, the Hunter is then cut down on the road.

With the Hunter dead, the last open question is whether the Duskhound will get away and carry a report back through the Gloomshaper's network. Robin and Greymalkin launch into pursuit at magical speed, sweep Celyn into the chase, and keep pressure on the hound while Izgil falls more slowly back toward the field and Viepuck works around the still-flying queen problem. The chase stays just close enough for repeated blows until Celyn finally kills the fleeing Duskhound, after which the party regroups and rapidly assesses how much danger remains if Lord Umbraeth notices the silence.

Once the party has a moment to breathe, it explains to the queen that the roadside victory has not really ended the problem so long as the Feywild portal remains open and useful to Lord Umbraeth's servants. Celyn states plainly that permanently closing the portal is the only practical way to thwart the threat for any meaningful length of time, while Robin makes no effort to hide how tired he is of fey disasters. Elaine II accepts the answer immediately and asks them to undertake the job, so the new plan becomes getting her safely into Cleenseau, stopping briefly in Asineau, gathering what little more they can from their research, sleeping, and striking the portal early the next morning.
